steps.title: Join the clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: Saville is not the protagonist, and she is the "occasion" of the novel. The conjunctive adverb "rather" introduces the second clause.

steps.title: Punctuate rather
steps.content: Choice A fuses the clauses with no punctuation. Choice B creates a comma splice. Choice D also uses only a comma before "rather." The remaining choice uses a semicolon before "rather" and a comma after it. The answer is C.
